title: Your cycles are running short
body: Your average cycle is running short. Cycles under 21 days are worth mentioning to a doctor — it is a common and treatable pattern.

title: Your cycles are running long
body: Your average cycle is running long. Cycles over 35 days can have a few different causes — a doctor can help figure out what is going on.

title: Your cycle length varies a lot
body: Your cycles vary by more than a week from month to month. This is fairly common but worth a chat with a doctor if it has been going on a while.

title: Your periods are running long
body: Periods consistently over 7 days are worth mentioning to a doctor. There are several common treatable causes.

title: You have been logging cramps
body: A few things that can help: heat on your lower abdomen, ibuprofen taken with food at the first sign (not after), and magnesium-rich foods like dark chocolate and nuts.

title: Persistent fatigue this phase
body: Fatigue around your period is common. Iron-rich foods, staying hydrated, and not skipping sleep all help. If it feels disabling it is worth mentioning to your doctor.

title: Bloating logged consistently
body: Hormonal bloating before and during your period is very common. Reducing salt, eating smaller meals, and staying hydrated can help ease it.

title: Sleep disruption logged
body: Progesterone fluctuations can disrupt sleep in the luteal and menstrual phases. A consistent bedtime, cooler room, and avoiding screens before sleep all help.

title: Brain fog logged consistently
body: Difficulty concentrating around your period is linked to hormone shifts, not your capability. Breaking tasks into smaller steps and building in rest helps.
